---
title: "BIOL 275 Lab 9 Contingency Analysis"
format:
  pdf:
    output-file: lab-09-worksheet.pdf
    pdf-engine: xelatex
    fontsize: 11pt
    geometry:
      - letterpaper
      - margin=0.6in
    number-sections: false
    toc: false
header-includes:
  - |
    \usepackage[most]{tcolorbox}
    \usepackage{enumitem}
    \setlist[itemize]{nosep, topsep=8pt, leftmargin=*}
    \setlist[enumerate]{nosep, topsep=8pt, leftmargin=*}
    
    % Pandoc tight lists: force list spacing
    \providecommand{\tightlist}{%
      \setlength{\itemsep}{0pt}%
      \setlength{\parskip}{0pt}%
      \setlength{\parsep}{0pt}%
    }
    
    \pagestyle{empty}
    
    % Compact title without titling.sty
    \makeatletter
    \renewcommand{\maketitle}{
      \begin{center}
        {\Large\bfseries \@title \par}
        \vspace{4pt}
        \end{center}
      \vspace{-1pt}
    }
    \makeatother
    
    % Tighten section spacing (template-safe; no titlesec)
    \makeatletter
    \renewcommand\section{\@startsection{section}{1}{\z@}%
      {0.2\baselineskip}%
      {0.2\baselineskip}%
      {\normalfont\large\bfseries}}
    \makeatother

    % Answer box styles
    \newtcolorbox{answerbox}[1][]{
      enhanced,
      sharp corners,
      boxrule=0.6pt,
      colback=white,
      colframe=black,
      left=6pt,right=6pt,top=6pt,bottom=6pt,
      #1
    }

    % Convenience commands for fixed-height boxes
    \newcommand{\ab}[1]{\begin{answerbox}[height=#1]\end{answerbox}}
---

\vspace{-0.15in}

\begin{answerbox}
\textbf{Name:} \hspace{2.5in} \textbf{Date:} \hspace{1.4in} \textbf{LA Initials:} \hspace{0.8in}
\end{answerbox}

## Dataset and Variables

**1. What is the name of the sleep variable? The depression variable? The variable that serves as the unique identifier for individuals?**
\ab{1in}

## Part I — Prepare the Data

**2. How many rows are there in the depression dataset (after removing rows with missing values)?**
\ab{0.5in}

**3. How many rows are there in the sleep dataset (after removing rows with missing values)?**
\ab{0.5in}

**4. How many rows are there in the joined dataset?**
\ab{0.5in}

**5. Draw the contingency table here. (two columns, labeled; two rows, labeled)**
\ab{2.5in}

\newpage

## Part 3 — Estimate Relative Risk

**6. What is the risk of depressive symptoms in short sleepers? Long sleepers?**
\ab{0.5in}

**7. What is the relative risk? Interpret your result in a complete sentence.**
\ab{1.5in}

## Part 4 — Test for Independence

**8. Write the null and alternative hypotheses in words.**
\ab{1.5in}

**9. Write the $\chi^2$ test statistic, $P$-value, and whether you reject or fail to reject the null hypothesis.**
\ab{1in}

**10.Can we conclude that short sleep causes depression? Why or why not?**
\ab{2in}
